Data collected: symptoms, reason for contact, main concern, eligibility, prior visits, remote photos/vitals
Roles handling data: patient, caregiver, online triage, care navigator, CHW
Tools/forms/scores used: NHS 111, online consultation forms, screening invitations, HWC empanelment
Common weak points / black zones: digital exclusion; one-symptom framing; patient may not know red flags
Product-design implication: Support free-text + structured symptom capture, accessibility, prior-visit import and escalation.

Data collected: physical arrival time, mode of arrival, obvious distress, reason for visit
Common weak points / black zones: occult waits; distress visible before registration not captured
Product-design implication: Record true arrival and pre-registration risk observations.

Data collected: demographics, ID/NHS number/insurance, contact info, reason for visit
Common weak points / black zones: administrative bottleneck before clinical review
Product-design implication: Add red-flag prompt and escalation path at registration.

Data collected: structured symptom answers, duration, severity, location, photos, patient concerns
Tools/forms/scores used: NHS 111, GP online consultation, UTC digital triage
Common weak points / black zones: form cannot capture nuance; capped requests; language/literacy gaps
Product-design implication: Allow 'not captured' signals, translator support and clinician escalation.

Data collected: chief complaint, acuity, red flags, pain/distress, prior risk, resource prediction
Common weak points / black zones: triage is risk sorting, not diagnosis; training variability
Product-design implication: Keep diagnostic uncertainty and repeated-visit flags separate from acuity.

Data collected: BP, HR, RR, temperature, SpO2, weight, pain, mental status, early warning score
Tools/forms/scores used: urgent-care vital signs policy, early warning scores, HEARTS BP protocols
Common weak points / black zones: vitals delayed/incomplete; no trend comparison
Product-design implication: Require repeat abnormal vitals and trend across encounters.

Data collected: history, physical exam, differential, red flags, patient agenda
Roles handling data: GP, ED clinician, UTC clinician, telehealth clinician
Common weak points / black zones: history/exam shortcuts; telehealth exam limits; symptoms dismissed
Product-design implication: Prompt history completeness, exam-needed flags and patient concern acknowledgement.

Data collected: past diagnoses, meds, allergies, family history, prior cancer/rare disease, prior tests
Common weak points / black zones: history exists but not surfaced; family history missing
Product-design implication: Longitudinal risk summary and high-risk history banners.

Data collected: focused exam findings, mental status, pain site, visible signs, neurologic deficits
Tools/forms/scores used: exam template, remote visual exam if telehealth
Common weak points / black zones: remote exams omit auscultation/private areas; repeated remote visits
Product-design implication: Remote-to-in-person conversion for unresolved or exam-dependent problems.

Data collected: test order, indication, priority, patient attendance
Tools/forms/scores used: EHR order, HWC/NCD screening, HEARTS protocols
Common weak points / black zones: patient does not attend; order/result mismatch; no owner
Product-design implication: Closed-loop from order to attended to result reviewed.

Data collected: clinical question, modality, body site, urgency, prior imaging
Common weak points / black zones: wrong/unclear clinical question; scan delayed/not read
Product-design implication: Structured clinical question and critical-finding workflow.

Data collected: normal/abnormal result, trend, critical value, reviewer, action
Tools/forms/scores used: practice results protocol, screening result systems
Common weak points / black zones: normal-test trap; abnormal result filed without action; trend missed
Product-design implication: Result-to-action field with owner and due date; trend visualization.

Data collected: diagnosis, uncertainty, differential, patient understanding, safety-net instructions
Common weak points / black zones: false reassurance; uncertainty not documented
Product-design implication: Document 'working diagnosis' vs 'ruled out' and patient-facing uncertainty plan.

Data collected: reason, target service, priority, previous tests, referral status
Common weak points / black zones: wrong specialty; more info request lost; referral assumed complete
Product-design implication: Referral state machine visible to patient/referrer.

Data collected: diagnosis, pending tests, red flags, follow-up plan, medications
Common weak points / black zones: pending results after discharge; GP not informed
Product-design implication: Discharge checklist with unresolved diagnostics and destination owner.

Data collected: symptom status, test/referral completion, treatment response, barriers
Common weak points / black zones: no one owns next step; patient must chase
Product-design implication: Follow-up tasks assigned to named owner with escalation.

Data collected: same symptom count, escalation/worsening, previous explanations, prior tests
Common weak points / black zones: visits separated across channels/clinicians; pattern invisible
Product-design implication: Automatic repeat-presentation detection and diagnostic review prompt.

Data collected: red flags, danger signs, unstable vitals, failed outpatient management
Tools/forms/scores used: red-flag lists, IMCI/iCCM danger signs, ESI
Common weak points / black zones: delayed transfer; transport barriers; non-clinical staff hesitation
Product-design implication: One-click escalation with reason and receiving-service handoff.

Data collected: referral question, prior tests, specialist assessment, diagnostic plan
Common weak points / black zones: insufficient information; advice-only without follow-up
Product-design implication: Structured pre-review data pack and safety-net output.

Data collected: chronic disease metrics, screening recalls, adherence, trend data, patient-reported outcomes
Common weak points / black zones: trend missed; patient drops out after screening; community notes not visible
Product-design implication: Longitudinal dashboards with overdue recalls and worsening trends.
